controller   \u2502  \u2502  \u2022 timelock      \u2502  \u2502 \u2502  \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518 \u2502  \u2022 add key     \u2502  \u2502  \u2022 suspend       \u2502  \u2502 \u2502  \u2502  \u2022 revoke     \u2502  \u2502  \u2022 revoke key  \u2502  \u2502  \u2022 reactivate    \u2502  \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518 Trust model:   - Each DID is uniquely mapped to a controller address on registration.   - Recovery uses a hash-committed scheme: the recovery hash is set at     registration and can only be exercised by proving pre-image knowledge.   - A 48-hour timelock protects recovery to give the controller time to     contest a malicious recovery attempt.   - PII never touches the chain \u2014 only keccak256 hashes of DIDs are stored.","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. NOTE: Don't confuse with {AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount}."}],"AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount(address,bytes32)":[{"details":"The `account` is missing a role."}],"EnforcedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is paused."}],"ExpectedPause()":[{"details":"The operation failed because the contract is not paused."}],"ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall()":[{"details":"Unauthorized reentrant call."}]},"events":{"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"addAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32)":{"params":{"didHash":"The identity to add the key to","keyHash":"keccak256 hash of the public key material"}},"addDelegate(bytes32,address,uint64)":{"params":{"delegate":"The address being delegated to","didHash":"The identity granting delegation","duration":"How long the delegation remains valid (in seconds)"}},"batchRegister(bytes32[],address[],bytes32[])":{"params":{"controllers":"Array of controller addresses","didHashes":"Array of DID hashes","recoveryHashes":"Array of recovery commitment hashes"}},"cancelRecovery(bytes32)":{"params":{"didHash":"The DID whose recovery to cancel"}},"constructor":{"params":{"admin":"The initial admin address receiving DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE"}},"executeRecovery(bytes32)":{"params":{"didHash":"The DID to recover"}},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"incrementCredentialCount(bytes32)":{"params":{"didHash":"The identity to update"}},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"resolveByController(address)":{"params":{"controller":"The controller address to look up"},"returns":{"_0":"The DID hash, or bytes32(0) if unbound"}},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."}},"stateVariables":{"_authKeyCount":{"details":"didHash => count of authentication keys"},"_authKeys":{"details":"didHash => authentication key hash => active flag"},"_controllerToDid":{"details":"controller address => didHash (reverse lookup)"},"_delegates":{"details":"didHash => delegate address => expiry timestamp"},"_identities":{"details":"didHash => Identity record"}},"title":"ZeroID","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"MAX_AUTH_KEYS()":{"notice":"Maximum number of authentication keys per identity"},"MAX_BATCH_SIZE()":{"notice":"Maximum batch registration size"},"RECOVERY_TIMELOCK()":{"notice":"Timelock duration for recovery operations"},"addAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Register an authentication key for an identity"},"addDelegate(bytes32,address,uint64)":{"notice":"Add a delegate with an expiry for a given DID"},"batchRegister(bytes32[],address[],bytes32[])":{"notice":"Batch-register multiple identities in a single transaction"},"cancelRecovery(bytes32)":{"notice":"Cancel a pending recovery (only by current controller)"},"executeRecovery(bytes32)":{"notice":"Execute a pending recovery after the timelock has elapsed"},"incrementCredentialCount(bytes32)":{"notice":"Increment credential count for a DID (called by CredentialRegistry)"},"isAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Check whether an authentication key is valid"},"isValidDelegate(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"Check whether an address is a valid delegate for a DID"},"pause()":{"notice":"Pause all registration and recovery operations"},"resolveByController(address)":{"notice":"Resolve the DID hash bound to a controller address"},"revokeAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32)":{"notice":"Revoke an authentication key"},"revokeDelegate(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"Revoke a delegate"},"totalIdentities()":{"notice":"Total number of params.didHash: The identity to add the key to
params.keyHash: keccak256 hash of the public key material

params.duration: How long the delegation remains valid (in seconds)

params.didHash: The DID whose recovery to cancel

params.admin: The initial admin address receiving DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

params.controller: The controller address to look up

returns._0: The DID hash, or bytes32(0) if unbound

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

MAX_AUTH_KEYS().notice: Maximum number of authentication keys per identity

addAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Register an authentication key for an identity

addDelegate(bytes32,address,uint64).notice: Add a delegate with an expiry for a given DID

batchRegister(bytes32[],address[],bytes3.notice: Batch-register multiple identities in a single transaction

cancelRecovery(bytes32).notice: Cancel a pending recovery (only by current controller)

executeRecovery(bytes32).notice: Execute a pending recovery after the timelock has elapsed

incrementCredentialCount(bytes32).notice: Increment credential count for a DID (called by CredentialRegistry)

isAuthKey(bytes32,bytes32).notice: Check whether an authentication key is valid

isValidDelegate(bytes32,address).notice: Check whether an address is a valid delegate for a DID

pause().notice: Pause all registration and recovery operations

resolveByController(address).notice: Resolve the DID hash bound to a controller address
